project_summary.description: Remove the CLI terminal dashboard (`aitri-hub monitor`) and absorb Aitri Graph as a native Artifact Graph tab in the Aitri Hub web dashboard. The CLI becomes a setup-and-launch tool only; all monitoring and visualization happens in the browser.

product_analysis.north_star_kpi: Artifact graph renders for a selected project within 2 seconds of tab open, with zero separate tool switches required
product_analysis.jtbd: When I need to understand how my requirements, user stories, and test cases are connected across an Aitri project, let me see the full dependency graph inside Hub without opening a separate tool
product_analysis.guardrail_metric: Existing web dashboard tabs (Overview, Alerts, Coverage, Velocity, Activity, All) must render in ≤2s and function correctly after CLI removal

no_go_zone: No GitHub remote graph loading — graph reads local spec files only; remote URL projects show 'Graph not available for remote projects' in the Graph tab
No artifact editing from graph — graph is strictly read-only; no create, update, or delete actions on spec files
No migration of AITRI-GRAPH's standalone server.js, localStorage registry, or its own project management UI — only the rendering logic (normalizer, graph engine) is absorbed
No force-directed or 3D graph layouts — only dagre hierarchical DAG layout is supported in v1
No partial CLI monitor retained — `aitri-hub monitor` is fully removed, not deprecated with a flag; users are directed to `aitri-hub web`
No changes to the alerts engine — alert rules and evaluation logic are untouched
No CSS or styles imported from Aitri Graph — all visual styling uses Hub's existing design tokens and component styles; only graph rendering logic (normalizer + Cytoscape engine) is absorbed

user_personas.goal: Inspect how requirements map to test cases for a specific project without leaving the Hub dashboard
user_personas.pain_point: Currently must open a separate Aitri Graph server and re-register the project there — duplicated setup, context switch

user_personas.goal: Verify that all FRs have linked test cases before approving a phase, using the same tool the team already has open
user_personas.pain_point: No visual dependency map in Hub; must cross-reference JSON files manually or run a separate graph tool

functional_requirements.title: Remove `aitri-hub monitor` CLI command
functional_requirements.description: The `aitri-hub monitor` subcommand and its supporting ANSI terminal renderer (`lib/renderer/cli.js`) are deleted. The CLI entry point no longer recognizes the `monitor` argument. Users are directed to `aitri-hub web` for monitoring.
acceptance_criteria: Running `aitri-hub monitor` prints 'monitor removed — run `aitri-hub web` to open the dashboard' and exits with code 0
`lib/renderer/cli.js` is absent from the codebase; no ANSI terminal rendering code remains
`aitri-hub --help` no longer lists `monitor` as a valid command
`aitri-hub setup` and `aitri-hub web` continue to function correctly after the removal

functional_requirements.title: Artifact Graph tab in the web dashboard
functional_requirements.description: The web dashboard gains a 7th navigation tab labeled 'Graph'. The tab is visible in the tab bar at all supported viewport widths (≥768px) without requiring scroll. When active, it renders the artifact dependency graph for the selected project.
acceptance_criteria: Web dashboard renders 7 tabs; 'Graph' tab is visible in the tab bar at 768px viewport width without horizontal scroll
Clicking the 'Graph' tab activates it and renders the graph panel; no full page reload occurs
Graph tab is accessible via keyboard (Tab key cycles through all 7 tabs)

functional_requirements.title: Collector reads spec artifacts per project and includes them in dashboard.json
functional_requirements.description: The collector gains a new reader `readSpecArtifacts(projectPath)` that reads `spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json` and `spec/03_TEST_CASES.json` for each local project and includes their contents in that project's entry in dashboard.json. The React frontend reads spec data from dashboard.json via the existing `/data/dashboard.json` polling — no new API endpoint or nginx change required.
acceptance_criteria: Given a project with a valid spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json, when the Graph tab loads that project, then FR nodes are extracted and available for rendering within ≤1 second
Given a project with spec/03_TEST_CASES.json present, when the graph loads, then TC nodes are linked to their parent FR nodes using the test case's `requirement_id` field
Given a project with no spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json, when the graph loads, then an empty-state message 'No artifacts found for this project' is displayed and no JavaScript error occurs
Given a project registered with a remote URL (not a local path), when the Graph tab is opened, then a message 'Graph not available for remote projects' is shown

functional_requirements.title: Render artifact dependency graph as interactive DAG
functional_requirements.description: Artifact nodes (FRs at top tier, TCs at bottom tier) are rendered using Cytoscape.js with dagre hierarchical layout. The graph is pannable by drag and zoomable by mouse wheel. A fit-to-screen button resets the viewport.
acceptance_criteria: Graph renders using dagre top-to-bottom hierarchical layout with FR nodes above TC nodes
Graph renders within ≤2 seconds of project selection for a spec with up to 50 FRs and 150 TCs
User can pan the graph by dragging the canvas and zoom with the mouse wheel without any additional controls
A 'Fit to screen' button resets the viewport so all nodes are visible within the canvas bounds

functional_requirements.title: Color nodes by artifact approval status
functional_requirements.description: Each node in the graph is colored according to the artifact's approval status as read from the project's .aitri state: approved (green), in_progress (blue), pending (gray), drift (orange).
acceptance_criteria: Approved nodes render with background color #34D399; pending nodes with #94A3B8; in_progress nodes with #60A5FA; drift nodes with #FB923C
All four status colors meet a minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1 against the graph canvas background
A legend showing the 4 status colors and their labels is visible within the Graph tab panel

functional_requirements.title: Expand and collapse subtrees by node click
functional_requirements.description: Clicking a parent FR node toggles visibility of its descendant TC nodes. A collapsed node displays a badge showing the count of hidden children.
acceptance_criteria: Given a parent FR node with visible child TC nodes, when the user clicks the FR node, then all descendant TC nodes are hidden and the FR node shows a badge with the count of hidden children (e.g. '(3)')
Given a collapsed FR node with a children badge, when the user clicks it again, then all descendant nodes are restored to their layout positions
Collapse/expand transition completes within ≤200ms with no layout reflow of non-affected nodes

functional_requirements.title: Project selector within the Graph tab
functional_requirements.description: The Graph tab provides a dropdown listing all projects registered in Hub. Selecting a project loads its artifact graph. The selection persists across tab switches within the same browser session.
acceptance_criteria: Graph tab shows a dropdown populated with all projects from dashboard.json; dropdown renders within ≤500ms of tab activation
Selecting a project from the dropdown triggers graph load for that project within ≤2 seconds
Previously selected project is restored when the user navigates away and returns to the Graph tab within the same browser session (sessionStorage or React state)
If only one project is registered, it is auto-selected on tab open

user_stories.i_want: the terminal monitor command to be removed and redirected to the web dashboard
user_stories.so_that: there is a single monitoring surface and I'm not confused by two diverging interfaces

acceptance_criteria.given: a developer runs `aitri-hub monitor` after upgrading
acceptance_criteria.then: output reads 'monitor removed — run `aitri-hub web` to open the dashboard' and the process exits with code 0

user_stories.i_want: to see a Graph tab in the Hub web dashboard
user_stories.so_that: I can access artifact visualization without switching to a separate tool

acceptance_criteria.given: the Hub web dashboard is open at 768px viewport
acceptance_criteria.when: the user looks at the tab bar
acceptance_criteria.then: all 7 tabs including 'Graph' are visible without horizontal scroll

user_stories.i_want: the graph to load the project's spec files automatically
user_stories.so_that: I don't need to point the tool at files manually

acceptance_criteria.given: a project registered in Hub with spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json containing 10 FRs and spec/03_TEST_CASES.json containing 20 TCs
acceptance_criteria.when: the user selects that project in the Graph tab
acceptance_criteria.then: 10 FR nodes and 20 TC nodes are rendered with edges connecting TCs to their parent FRs within ≤2 seconds

acceptance_criteria.given: a project registered in Hub with no spec/ directory
acceptance_criteria.when: the user selects it in the Graph tab
acceptance_criteria.then: the message 'No artifacts found for this project' is displayed and no JavaScript error is thrown

user_stories.i_want: to pan and zoom the dependency graph
user_stories.so_that: I can navigate large specs with many FRs and TCs

acceptance_criteria.given: a graph with 30 FR nodes is rendered
acceptance_criteria.when: the user drags the canvas and scrolls the mouse wheel
acceptance_criteria.then: the graph pans and zooms fluidly with no layout recalculation

user_stories.i_want: to see at a glance which FRs are approved vs pending
user_stories.so_that: I can identify coverage gaps without reading raw JSON

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with FR-001 approved, FR-002 in_progress, and FR-003 pending
acceptance_criteria.then: FR-001 node is #34D399, FR-002 node is #60A5FA, FR-003 node is #94A3B8, and a legend is visible

user_stories.i_want: to collapse test case nodes under an FR to reduce visual noise
user_stories.so_that: I can focus on the FR structure when the graph is large

acceptance_criteria.given: FR-005 has 8 visible child TC nodes
acceptance_criteria.when: the user clicks the FR-005 node
acceptance_criteria.then: all 8 TC nodes are hidden, FR-005 shows badge '(8)', and the transition completes in ≤200ms
all 8 TC nodes are restored to their dagre layout positions

user_stories.i_want: to switch between projects within the Graph tab
user_stories.so_that: I can compare artifact structures across projects without navigating away

acceptance_criteria.when: the user opens the Graph tab and clicks the project dropdown
acceptance_criteria.then: all 3 project names appear in the dropdown within ≤500ms

acceptance_criteria.given: the user selected 'Project B' in the Graph tab and navigated to the Overview tab
acceptance_criteria.when: the user clicks back to the Graph tab
acceptance_criteria.then: 'Project B' is still selected and its graph is displayed

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Graph tab must render a spec with up to 50 FRs and 150 TCs within 2 seconds of project selection
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Measured from project dropdown selection to first paint of all nodes: ≤2000ms in Chrome 120+ on a machine with 8GB RAM

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Missing or malformed spec files must not crash the Graph tab or any other Hub tab
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Given spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json contains invalid JSON, the Graph tab shows an error message and all other tabs continue rendering correctly; no unhandled JavaScript exception is thrown

non_functional_requirements.requirement: CLI removal must not break existing `aitri-hub setup` or `aitri-hub web` workflows
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: `aitri-hub setup` completes project registration successfully and `aitri-hub web` starts the dashboard after the `monitor` command and its renderer are removed; verified by running both commands end-to-end

constraints: Cytoscape.js and cytoscape-dagre added as npm devDependencies in web/package.json — bundled by Vite; zero new CLI runtime dependencies
Spec data flows exclusively through dashboard.json — the frontend has no direct filesystem access and no new endpoints are added to the server
All graph rendering happens client-side in the browser — no server-side graph computation
Graph node colors and all visual styling use Hub's existing CSS tokens — no styles imported or copied from Aitri Graph
technology_preferences: Graph engine: Cytoscape.js 3.x + cytoscape-dagre installed as npm devDependencies, bundled by Vite
Graph normalizer: logic adapted from Aitri Graph's normalizer.js (FR/TC JSON → Cytoscape nodes/edges) — rewritten as a plain JS module with no external dependencies
Spec data source: dashboard.json extended by collector's new readSpecArtifacts reader — same polling mechanism the frontend already uses
Project selector: React controlled dropdown using existing dashboard.json project list
